patch_description:
This crossover-based update adopts the CRX ring and CRY hub entanglement motif (as inspired by the higher-performing variant) while preserving the rest of the fixed training setup (data generation, feature map, re-uploading pattern, and training loop). The ANSATZ_SPEC now uses:
- Pre-entanglement RZ rotations with symmetry-based parameter sharing
- RY rotations with symmetry-based parameter sharing
- Ring entanglement via CRX on the outer ring (shared angle)
- Hub entanglement via CRY from center to edge qubits (shared hub-edge angle)
- Post-entanglement RZ rotations with symmetry-based parameter sharing

This combination aims to improve validation/test performance by leveraging the parameter-efficient CRX/CRY motif while maintaining adjacency constraints and the same parameter budget. The code preserves the original I/O contract and EVOLVE-BLOCK semantics, only changing the ANSATZ_SPEC inside EVOLVE-BLOCK.
